title: parse_proxy_addr doesn't strip path component
description: The parse_proxy_addr function at transport.rs:403-415 doesn't strip the path component from the proxy URL before parsing the port. If a user provides 'socks5://127.0.0.1:9050/' (with trailing slash) or other path component, the rsplit_once(':') at line 409 will include the path in port_str and fail to parse. Compare to parse_relay_addr which correctly strips the path on line 430 using split('/').next(). This will cause connection failure with misleading 'port is not a valid number' error. The fix: strip path before rsplit_once, similar to parse_relay_addr line 430.
